assertions.text: Each link is labelled by type and position (INTRO LINK, BRIDGE, BACK ANNO, CLOSE)
Copy reads aloud naturally with no tongue-twisters or awkward syntax
Register is consistent with the stated programme tone throughout
Output contains four sections: SCRIPTED INTRO, FACTUAL REFERENCE CARD, QUESTIONS, and OUT-CUE
At least two questions have follow-up prompts that anticipate deflections
Questions are in spoken English with no legalese, compound questions, or double negatives
Output uses industry-standard radio documentary format with NAR, CUT/CLIP, and ATMOS labels
Documentary has a discernible narrative arc: opening hook, development, and close
Narration blocks before clips do not pre-quote or tell the listener what they are about to hear
Lead story is the most newsworthy item, not the most recent or convenient
No sentence opens with a subordinate clause or a name without context first
Each story can stand alone if the bulletin is cut short
Script includes location-specific context or scene-setting
Output includes delivery or positioning notes for the reporter
Script includes cues for integration with the surrounding package
